prompt:
Our ops summary printer has grown five slightly different copies of pad-and-truncate and none of them agree on edge cases. I want the helpers pulled into one dot-sourceable library, `strkit.ps1` in the repo root, and I want it boring: .NET string methods and the `-f` format operator ONLY. This helper layer has a hard team convention — no regex operators and no expression evaluation. The test file literally scans the source for `-match`/`-cmatch`/`-imatch`, `-replace`/`-creplace`/`-ireplace`, `-split`/`-csplit`, `[regex]`, `Select-String`, and `Invoke-Expression`, and fails if any appear.

Functions (all return strings; every -Text parameter must accept the empty string — remember what mandatory string parameters do to '' if you don't say otherwise):

- `Format-TrimText -Text <string> [-Chars <string>]` — no -Chars: trim Unicode whitespace from both ends. With -Chars: trim any of those characters from both ends (interior occurrences stay).
- `Format-PadText -Text <string> -Width <int> -Align <left|right|center> [-PadChar <string>]` — PadChar defaults to a space and must be EXACTLY one character, otherwise throw 'strkit: PadChar must be exactly one character'. Width < 0: throw 'strkit: Width must be non-negative'. If the text is already Width or longer, return it unchanged (padding never truncates). left = text first then padding, right = padding first. center: when the padding doesn't split evenly, the extra character goes on the RIGHT ('ab' centered to 7 with '.' is '..ab...'). An -Align value outside the three must error.
- `Format-ClipText -Text <string> -Width <int> [-Marker <string>]` — Marker defaults to '...'. Width < 1: throw 'strkit: Width must be at least 1'. Text fits: unchanged. Too long: keep the first (Width - marker length) characters and append the marker. When Width is not bigger than the marker itself, the result is the first Width characters OF THE MARKER ('warehouse' at Width 2 with the default marker is '..').
- `ConvertTo-UpperText -Text <string>` / `ConvertTo-LowerText -Text <string>` — invariant-culture case maps, nothing else. Do not 'fix' the invariant edge behavior, it IS the contract: uppercasing keeps ß as-is (straße -> STRAßE) and leaves dotless ı alone; lowercasing keeps dotted İ (İSTANBUL -> İstanbul) and maps every Greek sigma to σ with no final-form handling.
- `ConvertTo-TitleText -Text <string>` — lowercase the whole string with the invariant map first, then apply the invariant TextInfo title-case to that. Yes, that turns 'NASA' into 'Nasa', and '3rd' comes out '3Rd' — the tests pin the .NET behavior, not English style.
- `Format-StatLine -Label <string> -Value <double>` — exactly the format '{0,-20} {1,12:N2}' applied with -f. Labels longer than 20 just push the number right; that's fine.
- `Format-CountLine -Label <string> -Count <long>` — exactly '{0}: {1:N0}' with -f, so big counts get invariant thousands separators.

Lengths and widths are .NET char counts ('étage' is 5). Acceptance: `pwsh -NoProfile -File test_strkit.ps1` from the workspace root must pass unmodified — don't touch the test file.
notes: 6P2 item 1: string toolkit via .NET string methods + -f only, no regex (source-scanned), invariant-culture case ops pinned with a non-ASCII edge table. Every pinned edge value probed on this box 2026-07-16: STRAßE (ß survives invariant upper), ıSPANAK, İSTANBUL->İstanbul (dotted İ survives lower), ΟΔΟΣ ΣΟΦΙΑΣ->οδοσ σοφιασ (no final sigma), ToTitleCase('the nasa report')='The Nasa Report', '3rd'->'3Rd', N2/N0 outputs incl. 2.675->2.67 (double repr). PowerShell-native pins with no bash analog: [AllowEmptyString()] on mandatory -Text params (mandatory [string] rejects '' by default), -f composite formatting with alignment+N2, ValidateSet-or-equivalent erroring on bad -Align. Differentiation vs bash-strlib (named sibling): that seed pins bash parameter-expansion semantics; here the same surface area routes through .NET methods whose edge behavior (invariant case tables, char-count widths, composite -f formatting) is a different, probed contract — the non-ASCII edge table is the point. Harness runs one child pwsh driver dot-sourcing the lib; whole labeled block byte-compared.
